title: Views, Triggers, Reusable Database Logic, and Boundaries · Data Contract Brief: Database Logic Boundary
summary: Multiple applications need a stable read contract and audited state transitions without hidden business behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql database logic boundary increment in a versioned read model and audited transition design. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Place constraints, derived data, transitions, and orchestration in database or application layers deliberately.
Predict trigger timing, row versus statement scope, recursion, ordering limits, and failure behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.

-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Publish a stable open_ticket_queue view and audit status changes for ticket 104. Expose no private member fields, prevent recursive trigger behavior, and prove rollback removes both visible and audit effects. A defensible solution applies Database Logic Boundary, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a versioned read model and audited transition design.

content.code:
CREATE TEMP VIEW open_ticket_queue AS
SELECT ticket_id, team_id, owner_id, severity, opened_at
FROM tickets
WHERE status <> 'resolved';
SELECT ticket_id, severity
FROM open_ticket_queue
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Database Logic Boundary evidence · Theory Database Logic Boundary
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-database-logic-boundary-tw8gym" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; view, trigger, reusable-logic, or side-effect evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Database Logic Boundary evidence · Theory Database Logic Boundary.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Database Logic Boundary into observable performance evidence. For Build Database Logic Boundary evidence · Theory Database Logic Boundary, keep Database Logic Boundary connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-database-logic-boundary-tw8gym
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
CREATE TEMP TRIGGER audit_status_tw8gym
AFTER UPDATE OF status ON tickets
BEGIN
  SELECT CASE WHEN NEW.status = OLD.status THEN RAISE(IGNORE) END;
END;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
